edges.description: Cytoskeletal and signaling disruption in chondrocytes impairs cartilage maturation and endochondral ossification.
Defective cartilage and ossification in the thorax and airway cartilage produce lethal respiratory compromise.

nodes.description: Vertebral developmental and ossification defects are recurrent radiographic findings in AOI.

evidence.reference_title: Mutations in two regions of FLNB result in atelosteogenesis I and III.
evidence.snippet: AOI and AOIII are autosomal dominant lethal skeletal dysplasias characterized by overlapping clinical findings that include vertebral abnormalities, disharmonious skeletal maturation, hypoplastic long bones, and joint dislocations.

evidence.reference_title: Prenatal diagnosis of atelosteogenesis type I at 21 weeks' gestation.
evidence.snippet: Fetal ultrasonography (US) revealed absent or deficient ossification of the posterior neural arches of the thoracic spine, humeri, radii, ulnae, fibulae, and short tubular bones other than the distal phalanges, in addition to extremely short, thick femora.

nodes.description: Distal digit shortening, especially involving digits 3-5, is reported in molecularly confirmed AOI.

evidence.reference_title: Clinical report: Two patients with atelosteogenesis type I caused by missense mutations affecting the same FLNB residue.
evidence.snippet: Both children had typical manifestations of AO type I, with severe rhizomelic shortening of the extremities, limited elbow and knee extension with mild webbing, pectus excavatum, broad thumbs with brachydactyly that was most marked for digits 3-5, dislocated hips and bilateral talipes equinovarus.

nodes.description: Broad thumbs are part of the distal limb malformation pattern in some molecularly confirmed cases.

evidence.reference_title: Clinical report: Two patients with atelosteogenesis type I caused by missense mutations affecting the same FLNB residue.
evidence.snippet: Both children had typical manifestations of AO type I, with severe rhizomelic shortening of the extremities, limited elbow and knee extension with mild webbing, pectus excavatum, broad thumbs with brachydactyly that was most marked for digits 3-5, dislocated hips and bilateral talipes equinovarus.

nodes.description: Cleft palate is reported in molecularly confirmed AOI and expands the craniofacial phenotype beyond limb and vertebral findings.

evidence.reference_title: Clinical report: Two patients with atelosteogenesis type I caused by missense mutations affecting the same FLNB residue.
evidence.snippet: Facial features included proptosis, hypertelorism, downslanting palpebral fissures, cleft palate, and retromicrognathia.

nodes.description: Affected neonates have severe short-limbed dwarfism.

evidence.reference_title: Identification of a de novo heterozygous missense FLNB mutation in lethal atelosteogenesis type I by exome sequencing.
evidence.snippet: Atelosteogenesis type I (AO-I) is a rare lethal skeletal dysplastic disorder characterized by severe short-limbed dwarfism and dislocated hips, knees, and elbows.

nodes.description: AOI cartilage shows abnormal chondrocyte clustering, giant chondrocytes, and incomplete ossification of cartilage anlagen. This represents impaired growth-plate chondrocyte maturation and endochondral replacement of cartilage by mineralized bone.

evidence.reference_title: Mutations in two regions of FLNB result in atelosteogenesis I and III.
evidence.snippet: Mutations in the gene encoding filamin B (FLNB) cause a spectrum of osteochondrodysplasias, including atelosteogenesis type I (AOI) and atelosteogenesis type III (AOIII).

evidence.reference_title: Mutations in two regions of FLNB result in atelosteogenesis I and III.
evidence.snippet: These results show that clustering of mutations in two regions of FLNB produce AOI/AOIII, and highlight the important role of this cytoskeletal protein in normal skeletogenesis.

evidence.reference_title: Prenatal ultrasonographic description and postnatal pathological findings in atelosteogenesis type 1.
evidence.snippet: Atelosteogenesis type 1 (AO1) is a rare lethal chondrodysplasia characterized by incomplete ossification of cartilage anlagen.

evidence.reference_title: Prenatal ultrasonographic description and postnatal pathological findings in atelosteogenesis type 1.
evidence.snippet: Histologically, the cartilage contains irregular clusters that occasionally include giant chondrocytes.

nodes.description: Downslanting palpebral fissures are part of the craniofacial phenotype in some molecularly confirmed cases.

evidence.reference_title: Clinical report: Two patients with atelosteogenesis type I caused by missense mutations affecting the same FLNB residue.
evidence.snippet: Facial features included proptosis, hypertelorism, downslanting palpebral fissures, cleft palate, and retromicrognathia.

nodes.description: Dominant missense FLNB variants associated with AOI alter full-length filamin B, an actin-binding cytoskeletal scaffold in cartilage. The abnormal protein perturbs actin organization and cell signaling in skeletal lineage cells rather than causing simple FLNB haploinsufficiency.

evidence.reference_title: Mutations in two regions of FLNB result in atelosteogenesis I and III.
evidence.snippet: The filamins are a family of cytoplasmic proteins that bind to and organize actin filaments, link membrane proteins to the cytoskeleton, and provide a scaffold for signaling molecules.

evidence.reference_title: Mutations in two regions of FLNB result in atelosteogenesis I and III.
evidence.snippet: The majority of the mutations resided in exon 2 and exon 3, which encode the CH2 domain of the actin-binding region of filamin B.

evidence.reference_title: Cell-Dependent Pathogenic Roles of Filamin B in Different Skeletal Malformations.
evidence.snippet: And in vitro studies showed that both variants led to a lack of filopodia and accumulation of the mutants in the perinuclear region in HEK293 cells.

evidence.reference_title: Cell-Dependent Pathogenic Roles of Filamin B in Different Skeletal Malformations.
evidence.snippet: FLNB variants differentially affect skeletal development which contributes to clinical heterogeneity of FLNB-related disorders.

evidence.reference_title: Mutations in two regions of FLNB result in atelosteogenesis I and III.
evidence.snippet: In this study, 14 novel missense mutations in FLNB were found in 15 unrelated patients with AOI and AOIII.

nodes.description: Absent or severely deficient fibular ossification is a recurrent severe limb abnormality in AOI.

evidence.reference_title: Prenatal diagnosis of atelosteogenesis type I at 21 weeks' gestation.
evidence.snippet: Fetal ultrasonography (US) revealed absent or deficient ossification of the posterior neural arches of the thoracic spine, humeri, radii, ulnae, fibulae, and short tubular bones other than the distal phalanges, in addition to extremely short, thick femora.

nodes.description: Hypertelorism contributes to the recognizable craniofacial phenotype in some molecularly confirmed cases.

evidence.snippet: Facial features included proptosis, hypertelorism, downslanting palpebral fissures, cleft palate, and retromicrognathia.

nodes.description: Congenital large-joint dislocations prominently affect the hips, knees, and elbows.

evidence.reference_title: Identification of a de novo heterozygous missense FLNB mutation in lethal atelosteogenesis type I by exome sequencing.
evidence.snippet: Atelosteogenesis type I (AO-I) is a rare lethal skeletal dysplastic disorder characterized by severe short-limbed dwarfism and dislocated hips, knees, and elbows.

nodes.description: Mandibular hypoplasia is part of the craniofacial phenotype in some molecularly confirmed cases.

evidence.snippet: Facial features included proptosis, hypertelorism, downslanting palpebral fissures, cleft palate, and retromicrognathia.

nodes.description: Pectus excavatum is reported in molecularly confirmed AOI.

evidence.snippet: Both children had typical manifestations of AO type I, with severe rhizomelic shortening of the extremities, limited elbow and knee extension with mild webbing, pectus excavatum, broad thumbs with brachydactyly that was most marked for digits 3-5, dislocated hips and bilateral talipes equinovarus.

nodes.description: Marked flattening of vertebral bodies is a key radiographic feature supporting AOI within the FLNB-related lethal skeletal dysplasia spectrum.

nodes.description: A prominent forehead is part of the craniofacial pattern in the severe FLNB-AOI spectrum.

evidence.snippet: prominent forehead; hypertelorism; and proptosis.

evidence.snippet: Facial features included proptosis, hypertelorism, downslanting palpebral fissures, cleft palate, and retromicrognathia.

nodes.description: Pulmonary hypoplasia is a major contributor to severe perinatal respiratory compromise in AOI.

evidence.reference_title: Prenatal diagnosis of atelosteogenesis type I at 21 weeks' gestation.
evidence.snippet: Fetal magnetic resonance imaging (MRI) using an ultrafast imaging sequence depicted dysmorphic features, pulmonary hypoplasia, and large cisterna magna.

evidence.reference_title: Prenatal ultrasonographic description and postnatal pathological findings in atelosteogenesis type 1.
evidence.snippet: Pulmonary hypoplasia is a characteristic finding that has been presumed to be the cause of neonatal lethality.

nodes.description: Molecularly confirmed cases show marked shortening of the proximal limb segments.

evidence.snippet: Both children had typical manifestations of AO type I, with severe rhizomelic shortening of the extremities, limited elbow and knee extension with mild webbing, pectus excavatum, broad thumbs with brachydactyly that was most marked for digits 3-5, dislocated hips and bilateral talipes equinovarus.

nodes.description: Bilateral clubfoot is reported in molecularly confirmed AOI.

evidence.reference_title: Clinical report: Two patients with atelosteogenesis type I caused by missense mutations affecting the same FLNB residue.
evidence.snippet: Both children had typical manifestations of AO type I, with severe rhizomelic shortening of the extremities, limited elbow and knee extension with mild webbing, pectus excavatum, broad thumbs with brachydactyly that was most marked for digits 3-5, dislocated hips and bilateral talipes equinovarus.

nodes.description: Small, hypoplastic thorax contributes to the lethal respiratory phenotype and helps distinguish AOI from milder FLNB-related skeletal dysplasias.

evidence.reference_title: FLNB-Related Disorders - GeneReviews® - NCBI Bookshelf
evidence.snippet: On prenatal ultrasound examination, the findings of FLNB-AO1 consist of thoracic hypoplasia and limb shortening with delayed or absent ossification of vertebral and appendicular elements.

nodes.description: Perinatal lethality reflects combined skeletal thoracic restriction, pulmonary hypoplasia, and primary upper-airway cartilage failure. In reported AOI neonates, severe subglottic hypoplasia and tracheomalacia allowed tracheal collapse and contributed to death independently of pulmonary hypoplasia.
nodes.id: Thoracic Insufficiency and Upper Airway Failure

evidence.reference_title: Prenatal ultrasonographic description and postnatal pathological findings in atelosteogenesis type 1.
evidence.snippet: Although pulmonary hypoplasia was present, it was moderate and considered unlikely to be the sole cause of death.

evidence.reference_title: Prenatal ultrasonographic description and postnatal pathological findings in atelosteogenesis type 1.
evidence.snippet: The marked luminal narrowing, tracheomalacia, and temporal proximity of extubation to demise support tracheal collapse as a major contributor to the death in AO1.

evidence.reference_title: Clinical report: Two patients with atelosteogenesis type I caused by missense mutations affecting the same FLNB residue.
evidence.snippet: Respiratory insufficiency with tracheal hypoplasia, laryngeal stenosis, and pulmonary hypoplasia have all been described in patients with AO type I and we conclude that compromised pulmonary function is a major contributor to morbidity and mortality in this condition.

nodes.description: Severe airway malacia is a documented contributor to respiratory failure and death in AOI.

evidence.reference_title: Prenatal ultrasonographic description and postnatal pathological findings in atelosteogenesis type 1.
evidence.snippet: Detailed neonatal and postmortem examination showed severe subglottic hypoplasia and tracheomalacia.
